content:
just got into astra reign and ive been using this team <:A_pinch:1432980538536493057> 
should i make any changes to wheels and such/characters

Personally, I don’t ever use the Ultra turn unless it’s basically handed to me. I primarily use Ultra because Winkle and Horla are good, not because of the realm mechanic — I generally see the absolute lack of a defensive mechanic in the realm mastery as a detriment. 

Because I rely on killing threatening enemies and either Devour or Tranquil Seas for most of my defensive mechanics, I tend to use at least one aequor character in my Ultra team (if I’m playing for the specific Ultra DOS in mind) and otherwise use one or two in a Caro team, so that I have more Devour options.

I think the biggest problem you generally need to solve is not being killed by your enemies extremely quickly, which means you need to be able to clear trash efficiently and burst really well (damage and shielding.) Sustain is almost always an issue because they don’t have an out of the box healer, and even if you’re using something like Clementine or Horla, healing is generally not something you brought them for, but more something that occasionally happens by pleasant surprise.

90% if these concerns come from being at a time where any and every incidental trash fight is a drain on your health and basically every elite is a life threatening problem, though. So adjusting your team with this in mind may actually just slow you down. Teambuilding in Morimens is highly context dependent.

Fauxborn would be your Main DPS since she is E3, while Corposant isn’t E3, but E2, making Corpo a Warden, not DPS.

I would give Fauxborn Goliath Sig + Dearest Friend, and give her the CoV “Medicine”(Crimsum Pulse works. April’s Praise if she is built completely). 

Corposant Passive scales with Death Res for more Dmg + Shield. I would give her “Burial Grounds” w/ DR WoDs, or “Feast from Afar” with Shield Amp WoDs. 

Tulu is fine.

Miryiam would be “Life Drain” holder. In this case, we ignore her Crit Dmg for scaling passive, and just give her 2 Keyflare WoDs.

Faint could be nice for Death Res Stacking.

Aigis gives Perma Vuln + Embryo Gen + One Turn Stun.

Jenkin for more Crit Rate from Rouse + Crit Dmg Stacking from Rouse combo w/ Exalt

Horla is universal with “Joy Stance” giving more Aliemus and Retaing Arithma between Turns. “Sad Stance” giving more Shielding and Healing. “Anger Stance” giving more DMG. Her Exalt is very good burst with “Anger Stance” for 20% Crit Rate and Crit Dmg. At E1, her Strike applies Vuln, and Defense Weak, for 1 Arithma #Value

The two teams you can build are around Fauxborn and Cantena. Both of them will want 3 more copies to be a powerful DPS, but both can work *for now*. 

Fauxborn team will be with Myriam/Celeste/Tulu/Murphy/Goliath.

Cantena team will be with Thais mandatory. Then Salvador/Aigis/Faint/Celeste.

Tawil needs 2 more copies to become a Main DPS.

Right now on the banner there is one more last weekly rotation for Anni Banner with 9 Awakeners. 

I recommend 4 Copies Kath, 4 Copies Pollux, 4 Copies Castor, 4 Copies Clem, 4 Copies Corposant. 

Pickman is solid at 1 Copy. Doreslain is weird, but if you like him, he needs 4 copies. Inferno is only if you got money to spend for 8 copies.

Guys what are the priority characters  to aim for gneotic advance and talent levels?
you're welcome

In my experience, characters who actually use their stats (DPS) or have a huge CON want the gnostic advance more, and usually by extension, the Soulforge talent improvement. Generally this is the attackers; characters that scale stuff like Aliemus Regen don't really care about levels as much as they care about copies, because they get more of their target stat from copies and not levels. Someone like Celeste doesn't really care at all; she scales her healing off of her stats, but juicing her healing isn't going to dramatically alter the outcome of an expedition like giving a steroid to Kath's ATK will, and Celeste's best use is giving arithmetica and a false death resist. 

More aliemus at the start of combat is obviously always good, but since it's so exorbitantly expensive, people tend to increase Madness Omen to their preferred amount.
Other than that, give resources to your favorites and characters who are actually good, not characters you dislike or that are not good

Poison is expensive to run, really expensive because of how deep you need to invest. 

However, poison can be run and fun, if you are early game and ignore end game poison stuff for now(unless you want to build for endgame and love poison).

Depends on Fauxborn versus Corpo Enlightenments, and WoDs. Either of them having DPS WoDs and dealing “most damage” should be correct. Fauxborn can be Sub-Dps/Support/DPS, and Corpo can be Warden/DPS. Murphy is Warden. Miryiam is Support.
